RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 130-DB, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Donna Barnes, a Democrat, represents Raytown, parts of Kansas City and Jackson County (District 28) in the Missouri House of Representatives. She was elected to her first two-year term in November 2024.
Barnes graduated from Southeast High School located in Kansas City and briefly attended UMKC and Penn Valley Community College. Prior to being elected, Barnes worked as a lead sales service associate for the United States Postal Service for 35 years in the Kansas City area.
Outside of legislative duties, Barnes enjoys serving her community and the children at her church. She is a Jackson County Election Judge and a Jackson County Brooking Committeewoman. Her interests also include being a sports enthusiast, reading, getting outside to walk, traveling, and spending time with her family.
Barnes is married to former House Representative Jerome Barnes. Together, they have three children, a granddaughter, and a lovely daughter-in-law.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Changes the age when a person may be excused from serving on a petit and grand jury
Establishes the offenses of harassment of a school or recreation athletic official and entry or remaining on site of a school or recreation athletic contest after being forbidden
Expands the definition of special victim to include sports officials at a sporting event
Creates provisions relating to communication access services
Designates the week of November each year as "Veterans Week of Service"

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Establishes the "Firefighters' Procedural Bill of Rights Act"
Modifies provisions relating to mail sent by state agencies
Modifies provisions relating to the protection of children
Establishes a program that provides wellness checks for at-risk veterans
Provides federal Servicemember Civil Relief Act (SCRA) protections to National Guard members called to state duty
Specifies that the department of health and senior services shall not deny trauma center status to a hospital based on its distance from another trauma center
Modifies provisions relating to qualifications for public office
Specifies that the department of health and senior services shall not deny trauma center status to a hospital based on its distance from another trauma center
Modifies provisions relating to the age of marriage
Modifies provisions governing city-county library boards of trustees in St. Charles County
Allows firefighters to initiate the towing of vehicles in order to clear an accident scene
Modifies provisions relating to boating safety identification cards
Provides for the distribution of certain moneys for a memorial or museum dedicated to veterans in Perry County
